_meta.purpose: Scores apps NOT in known_signatures.json against permission/behavior patterns observed across the 14-app corpus. Exists because operators reskin under new names faster than any static database can track (see known_reskin_operators in known_signatures.json).
_meta.scoring: Sum matched signal weights. threshold_review >= 30 shows a soft in-app notice. threshold_alert >= 55 shows the full alert UI.
_meta.false_positive_note: Legitimate apps (parental controls with disclosed operation, accessibility tools, MDM/enterprise device managers, Find My Device) can trigger several of these signals individually. This model flags for HUMAN REVIEW by the device owner -- it never auto-removes anything, and always shows which specific signals fired so the user can judge context (e.g. 'I run an enterprise MDM app, that's expected').

signals.detection: App is installed (present in PackageManager) but has no enabled launcher activity / icon visible in the app drawer.
signals.rationale: Present in 12/14 corpus apps' own marketing copy as an explicit selling point ('100% invisible', 'no icon', 'stays hidden').
Core data-capture bundle for the overt_stalkerware and partner_trust taxonomy categories.

signals.detection: Holds ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION together with either CAMERA or RECORD_AUDIO.
signals.rationale: Ambient surveillance combination (ambient recording, remote camera activation) -- present in FlexiSPY, SpyBubble Pro, uMobix marketing.

signals.detection: Requests BIND_ACCESSIBILITY_SERVICE without being a known accessibility tool (screen reader, etc. -- cross-checked against a small allowlist).
signals.rationale: Used by several corpus apps for keylogging and screen-content capture beyond what standard permissions allow.
Needed to keep a hidden background service alive continuously -- common but not sufficient alone (many legitimate apps also request this, hence low weight).

signals.detection: Registers as a Device Administrator app AND is not a known enterprise MDM package.
signals.rationale: Used to prevent the device owner from uninstalling the app without extra steps -- a consent-architecture red flag matching this project's rubric's score-0 definition.

signals.detection: App label matches a list of common disguise names (e.g. 'System Update', 'Play Services', 'Sync', 'Wi-Fi Service') while the underlying package metadata / developer signature does not match Google/AOSP known signing keys.
signals.rationale: Reported disguise pattern for uMobix and others in independent reviews read during corpus verification.

signals.detection: Outbound network traffic (from on-device VPN-based traffic inspection, opt-in) matches a domain in known_signatures.json's known_domains list.
signals.rationale: Direct match to the verified corpus -- highest-confidence signal when available. Requires the user to opt in to local traffic inspection (a on-device VPN service, data never leaves the device except the aggregate report described in reporting_client.md).

explicit_non_signals.note: Signals intentionally NOT included, and why -- to avoid over-flagging and to stay consistent with this project's rubric distinguishing good-faith disclosed monitoring from covert monitoring.
excluded: Simple presence of ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION alone (used by thousands of legitimate apps)
Presence of a 'parental control' or 'family' keyword in the app name (Bark and Qustodio, both baselines, use these terms too)
Any signal based on who installed the app or their stated intent -- this tool only evaluates technical behavior, never assumes motive
